name: Cavern Library
description: A vast underground library carved into the cavern walls. Bookshelves stretch from floor to ceiling, stuffed with texts so ancient they predate the concept of an index. The air is thick with dust, knowledge, and the faint smell of scholarly despair. Several shelves have collapsed entirely, their contents scattered across the floor in an academic avalanche. A reading desk sits in the center, illuminated by a cluster of glowing crystals embedded in the ceiling.

responses.look: Rows of bookshelves crammed with texts in languages that range from 'archaic' to 'possibly invented.' Titles include 'A Bureaucrat's Guide to Immortality,' 'Mushroom Patterns and Their Meanings,' and 'How to Answer Guardian Questions Without Dying (Third Edition).' Several are bookmarked at suspiciously relevant pages.
responses.use: You browse the shelves. Most texts are incomprehensible, but one book -- 'Mushroom Patterns' -- contains a diagram showing a specific sequence: left, right, center, left, center. It's circled in ancient ink. Someone wanted this pattern remembered.
responses.take: You try to remove a book. The shelf groans. Removing structural literature is inadvisable.
responses.default: You survey the library. It's the kind of place where knowledge goes to retire.

responses.look: A stone reading desk lit by ceiling crystals. An open book lies on it, turned to a page about the Guardian Chamber: 'The Guardian asks three questions. The first concerns the kingdom's founding year. The second concerns the name of the first bureaucrat. The third... the page is torn. Of course it is.
responses.use: You sit at the desk and read. The open book reveals clues about the guardian's test. Founding year: 847. First bureaucrat: Aldric the Fastidious. Third question: missing. You'll need to find that answer elsewhere.
responses.default: You examine the desk. Someone has been studying here recently. The dust is disturbed in a pattern that suggests frantic research.

responses.look: A section of shelving that has collapsed, spilling books and broken wood across the floor. Among the debris, several intact wooden planks could be salvaged. The books themselves are ruined -- water damage from an underground seep has turned most to pulp.
responses.use: You rummage through the debris. The books are unsalvageable, but the wooden shelving planks are solid -- thick oak that hasn't rotted despite centuries underground. These could be useful.
responses.take: The collapsed shelves are too large to take whole, but individual planks could be salvaged.
responses.default: You survey the destruction. The library's loss is potentially someone's structural gain.

responses.look: A stoppered bottle of ink on the reading desk. The ink is black as night and slightly iridescent. The label reads 'Official Bureaucratic Ink -- Expires: Never.' It has opinions, apparently.
responses.use: The ink needs something to write on. A form, perhaps. An important, bureaucratic form.

responses.look: Solid oak planks from the collapsed shelving. Thick, sturdy, and still in good condition despite their age. They'd be useful for building or repairing something that needs to float. Or not collapse.
responses.use: Planks are building material, not a finished product. You'd need something specific to build.

actions.text: You pocket the ancient ink. It's warm to the touch and slightly vibrating, as though the ink itself is eager to fill out paperwork. After centuries in a library, it's finally going to see some action. The ink is, genuinely, the most enthusiastic thing in the caverns.

You salvage several solid oak planks from the wreckage. The library's loss is your gain, though the books scattered around them seem to judge you for prioritizing lumber over literature. Pragmatism over education -- the adventurer's creed.

You find the mushroom pattern book. The diagram is clear: activate mushrooms in the sequence left, right, center, left, center. The book notes: 'This pattern reveals the Crystal Chamber viewing passage from the West Wing. Do NOT eat the mushrooms. This warning should not be necessary but historical data suggests otherwise.'

You study the open book carefully. The Guardian's questions: First -- 'In what year was the kingdom founded?' Answer: 847. Second -- 'What was the name of the first kingdom bureaucrat?' Answer: Aldric the Fastidious. Third -- the page is torn. The answer to the third question must be elsewhere in the caverns.

shelf-collapse.title: Death by Literature
shelf-collapse.narratorText: You climb the bookshelves. This is a library, not a gymnasium. The ancient shelving, which has been holding together through sheer literary stubbornness for centuries, finally gives way. An avalanche of books, wood, and accumulated dust buries you under the weight of knowledge -- literally. The irony is not lost on the narrator. You are crushed by the very texts that could have taught you not to climb unstable furniture. The library is, if nothing else, thorough in its lessons.

dynamicDescriptions.knows_mushroom_pattern: The cavern library hums with scholarly energy. You've found the mushroom pattern sequence -- left, right, center, left, center -- and the guardian's first two answers. The third answer remains tantalizingly absent, somewhere deeper in the caverns.

tiers: The bookshelves here contain more than just dust and scholarly despair. Somewhere among the collapsed literature is practical knowledge about bioluminescent fungi and the specific order in which they respond to being poked.
Use the bookshelves to find a book about mushroom patterns. It contains a diagram showing the activation sequence for the West Wing mushrooms: a specific left-right-center combination.
Use the bookshelves to read the mushroom pattern book. The sequence is left, right, center, left, center. You'll need this to open the Crystal Chamber passage in the West Wing.

The reading desk has a book open to a rather informative page. Someone was researching the Guardian's test questions before the curse interrupted their study session. Two out of three answers are here.

Use the reading desk to study the Guardian's questions. You'll learn the founding year of the kingdom and the name of the first bureaucrat. The third answer is missing -- you'll need to find it elsewhere in the caverns.
Use the reading desk to learn two answers: 847 (founding year) and Aldric the Fastidious (first bureaucrat). The third answer must be found in the echo chamber, south of the West Wing.
